briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for June 26, 2026. Today, we will primarily cover the severity of the record-breaking heatwave that has swept across Europe, along with updates on domestic and international efforts towards carbon neutrality and advancements in climate adaptation technologies.

sections.title: 🔴 Europe-wide Intense Heatwave and the Warning of Climate Change
sections.content: Recently, Europe has experienced a series of record-breaking heatwaves, sounding a severe warning about the direct impacts of climate change. Scientists state that Europe's unusual June heatwave would have been 'virtually impossible' without climate change, clearly indicating that human-induced climate change is intensifying these heatwaves [29, 38, 51, 54, 58, 59, 62, 71]. In Paris, France, alcohol sales were halted to support hospitals amidst the deadly heatwave, and in the UK, June heat records were broken for three consecutive days, marking an unprecedented situation [59, 64]. These extreme weather phenomena, coupled with population decline issues in the European Union's (EU) islands and coastal regions, pose a complex threat [1]. Furthermore, the economic damage is severe, with the German economy suffering billions of euros in losses due to the heatwave [6].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Efforts for Carbon Neutrality Implementation and Climate Crisis Response
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts to address the climate crisis and achieve carbon neutrality are becoming visible. A unified consultative body has been launched to remove barriers between institutions, and memoranda of understanding have been signed for joint responses, thereby strengthening cooperation systems for efficient climate crisis response [3, 4]. Pohang City demonstrated efforts to reflect citizen opinions in policy by holding pre-workshops for citizen review and implementation checks of carbon neutrality policies [12, 13, 20]. Pocheon City is also focusing on education and awareness-raising, promoting the establishment of a carbon neutrality education center utilizing disused schools [14]. Furthermore, the role of the financial sector is being emphasized, with the Korea Exchange hosting a policy forum on green transition and transition finance to discuss industrial competitiveness and carbon neutrality solutions [15, 21].

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy and Smart Technology-based Climate Adaptation
sections.content: In response to the era of climate crisis, efforts for renewable energy transition and smart technology-based adaptation are actively underway. SK Hynix is accelerating its net-zero implementation by sourcing 31% of its production electricity from renewable energy [25], and Samsung Electronics has achieved a 95% renewable energy transition rate. Major domestic companies are thus moving towards carbon neutrality goals through ESG management [26, 27]. In the agricultural sector, 'smart farming' technologies that overcome heatwaves and droughts are being demonstrated in the field, and Ho Chi Minh City is also promoting smart and eco-friendly rural development, indicating that technological solutions for climate change adaptation are being sought [8, 9]. Korea Water Resources Corporation (K-water) is driving energy transition in various ways, including promoting the expansion of renewable energy by holding public-private cooperation meetings to vitalize the floating solar power industry [24, 28].

sections.title: 🌍 Widespread Socio-Economic Impacts of Climate Change and International Discussions
sections.content: Climate change is having widespread socio-economic impacts, from energy security to social inequality and mental health, triggering various discussions in the international community. Discussions among international experts on the impact of climate change on nuclear power generation were held in Seoul [2], and climate change issues are being significantly addressed in political circles, with U.S. House candidates discussing climate change, the military, and housing issues through Q&A sessions [30]. Furthermore, direct health issues such as the impact of climate change on pollen levels and skin health are being raised [31, 33, 40], and the importance of mental health as both a consequence and a determinant of climate adaptation is also being emphasized [46]. The necessity for international cooperation and a comprehensive approach is being highlighted, with leaders from various countries urging that oceans be at the core of climate action [41, 55].

briefing.closing: As highlighted in today's briefing, climate change is having immediate and severe impacts globally, making domestic and international response efforts increasingly crucial. Continued interest and participation in scientific insights and concrete actions to overcome the climate crisis are essential. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_title: Trump clean energy tax credit cutoff drives project rush as prices set to soar - Reuters
articles.description: Trump clean energy tax credit cutoff drives project rush as prices set to soar Reuters

summary.phenomenon: A surge in clean energy projects is occurring following the Trump administration's announcement to cut off clean energy tax credits.
summary.cause: The Trump administration's policy to discontinue clean energy tax credits is identified as the cause of this project surge.
summary.outlook: Consequently, prices related to clean energy are projected to rise sharply.
